2606.20369.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
2606.20369.paper_summary: Online hate speech and misinformation frequently overlap, yet NLP research has mainly treated them in isolation. While LLMs represent a scalable solution for assisting humans in the generation of counterspeech for both threats, zero-shot models frequently generate repetitive and vague responses, underscoring the need for high-quality examples to steer model generation. However, existing counterspeech datasets against the overlap of hate and misinformation are scarce and limited to single-turn English dialogues, while real-life interactions span across multiple turns and languages. To bridge this gap, we introduce the first large-scale, expert-curated, multilingual dataset of dialogues tackling the intersection of hate and misinformation. To ensure factual grounding, the dialogues are also anchored in verified external knowledge (i.e., fact-checking articles and NGO reports) and include document- and chunk-level span annotations, making it directly applicable for RAG systems. Covering five languages and targeting hate directed at seven marginalized groups, this novel resource enables the training and evaluation of more persuasive, factually grounded counterspeech models.
2606.20369.paper_summary_zh: 線上仇恨言論和錯誤資訊經常重疊，然而自然語言處理研究主要將它們孤立對待。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）代表了一種可擴展的解決方案，幫助人類生成針對這兩種威脅的反駁言論，但零樣本模型經常生成重複且模糊的回應，突顯出需要高質量範例來引導模型生成。然而，現有針對仇恨和錯誤資訊重疊的反駁言論數據集稀缺，且僅限於單輪的英語對話，而現實生活中的互動則跨越多輪和多種語言。為了填補這一空白，我們介紹了第一個大規模、專家策劃的多語言對話數據集，專注於解決仇恨和錯誤資訊的交集。為了確保事實基礎，這些對話也基於經過驗證的外部知識（即事實查核文章和非政府組織報告），並包括文檔和片段級別的範圍標註，使其可直接應用於檢索增強生成（RAG）系統。這一新資源涵蓋五種語言，針對七個邊緣化群體的仇恨言論，使得訓練和評估更具說服力、事實基礎的反駁言論模型成為可能。
2606.20369.author: Helena Bonaldi et.al.
2606.20369.authors: Helena Bonaldi, Genoveffa Martone, Marco Guerini
